q: What are example (certified) queries in a Genie Space and when should you add one instead of relying on free-form generation?
a: In Databricks docs these are called example SQL queries or trusted assets — the UI labels verified ones with a 'Trusted' badge. An example query is a complete, vetted SQL statement paired with the natural-language question it answers. When a user's question closely matches the example's phrasing, Genie uses the verified SQL instead of generating from scratch; parameterized variants let users swap filter values and rerun while keeping the logic anchored. When responses marked as verified are returned in chat, users see extra confidence indicators. Add an example query when: (1) free-form generation consistently produces the wrong joins for a known high-value question; (2) a question involves complex multi-step logic that natural language alone can't reliably disambiguate; (3) you need deterministic answers for compliance-sensitive metrics. The ordering of effort: define SQL expressions first for business terms, add example queries for recurring complex patterns, and write text instructions only as a last resort. Don't add an example query for every possible question — over-curating kills the flexibility advantage of Genie.
fj: I'll add example queries for every question users might ask so Genie always gets it right.
fs: Over-curating with example queries turns Genie back into a fixed-question dashboard. Use them only for complex or high-value questions where free-form generation reliably fails. SQL expressions cover reusable business terms; example queries cover known hard patterns.

code.label: Example: parameterized revenue-by-region query
lines: -- 'What was revenue by region last quarter?'
